food_subgroup: Noodle dishes — dry ramen with protein accompaniment

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for dry ramen with dumplings. FOODON_00002187 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pasta and noodles) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for dry ramen with dumpling accompaniment.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific dish combination. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: egg (possible in dumpling wrapper or noodle)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of handmade dumpling dry ramen is approximately 380 g, comprising a portion of wheat ramen noodles tossed in specialty sauce accompanied by several handmade dumplings.

nutrient_source.title: Ramen noodles, cooked (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #20116) combined with pork dumplings / gyoza (USDA FoodData Central #168927) and estimated sauce contribution; weighted composite per serving ratio
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for handmade dumpling dry ramen as a composite dish. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of: (1) cooked wheat ramen noodles (USDA SR Legacy #20116, ~60% by weight), (2) pork/chicken dumplings/gyoza (USDA FoodData Central #168927, ~30% by weight), and (3) estimated sauce contribution (soy-sesame base, ~10% by weight). Per-100g values represent the blended composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — wheat noodles / Asian noodles analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for handmade dumpling dry ramen. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat-based Asian noodles (boiled), which reports GI values in the range 47–58 (mean ~52). The dumpling component and sauce are low-GI contributors. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 100.7) / 100 ≈ 52.4, rounded to 52.5. Serving carbohydrate estimated as 26.5 g/100g × 380 g serving = ~100.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100g; ~1,824 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content from soy-based sauce and dumpling seasoning; a single serving may provide over 75% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 52) but high glycemic load per serving (~52.5) due to large carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the GI of wheat ramen noodles is moderate-low, the large serving size results in a high glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from dumpling filling and sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content and moderate saturated fat from pork-filled dumplings and sesame-soy sauce present a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Handmade Dumpling Dry Ramen is a Japanese-fusion noodle dish served without broth, consisting of springy wheat-based ramen noodles tossed in a rich specialty sauce — typically a soy-sesame or umami-forward blend — accompanied by several handmade dumplings (gyoza-style) filled with seasoned pork or chicken. The dish is inspired by Japanese abura soba or mazemen (broth-free ramen) and is adapted for the Singapore restaurant market. The noodles are boiled to an al dente texture and then tossed hot in the sauce, absorbing the flavours of garlic, sesame oil, and soy. The dumplings are typically pan-fried or steamed and served alongside or atop the noodles. The overall flavour profile is savoury, umami-rich, and slightly nutty from sesame, with a satisfying chewy texture from both the noodles and dumpling wrappers. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-to-high calorie meal (~703 kcal per 380 g serving) with a meaningful protein contribution from the dumpling filling (~29.6 g per serving) and a high carbohydrate load from the noodles (~100.7 g per serving). Sodium content is elevated due to soy-based sauce, making this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low-medium range for wheat noodles), but the glycemic load per serving is high, warranting portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. This dish is not suitable for gluten-free or vegetarian diets in its standard form.
common_user_queries: Dry ramen vs soup ramen nutrition
Is dry ramen high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked wheat ramen noodles and pork/chicken dumplings. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (boiled wheat Asian noodles, GI ~52). Confidence is medium due to the estimation methodology and restaurant-specific sauce variability.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked wheat ramen noodles (#20116) and pork dumplings/gyoza (#168927), weighted approximately 60:30:10 (noodles:dumplings:sauce). Actual values will vary by restaurant preparation, sauce recipe, and dumpling filling. 2. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as wheat noodles and standard dumpling fillings contain negligible industrial trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA source data. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the source database. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source estimate. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source estimate. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source estimate. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this dish type. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source estimate. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source estimate. 10. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (wheat-based Asian noodles); no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. 11. Halal status is listed as false as the dish may contain pork in the dumpling filling; restaurant-specific halal certification should be verified. 12. The dish is served at Flavours by Saute - Funan (field survey data source). 13. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese given the Japanese-fusion and Singapore restaurant context. Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local names are not applicable for this dish origin.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked wheat ramen noodles and pork dumplings with documented weighting methodology.
Estimated GI of 52 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for boiled wheat Asian noodles; documented estimation basis in gi_source.
Applied Singapore restaurant context multilingual naming: Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese provided.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes field.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency.
Set gi_category to 'low' per GI threshold rule (52 ≤ 55).
Used only approved sources: USDA FoodData Central and University of Sydney GI Database.
Ontology match_type values restricted to 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' only.
